name: Wasabi Hot Cloud Storage API
what_it_does: S3-compatible hot cloud object storage with no egress or API request fees, at $6.99/TB/month flat rate.
use_cases: Bulk object storage for backups, archives, and media at lower cost than AWS S3
Drop-in S3 replacement for agents already using boto3 or s3cmd
Storing large datasets for AI/ML pipelines without egress cost concerns
Offsite backup storage for databases and application data
Media asset storage with CDN integration via partner CDNs
not_for: Teams needing native AWS integrations (IAM roles, Lambda triggers, etc.)
Temporary or transient storage under 1TB where S3 free tier is sufficient
Compliance regimes requiring FedRAMP or ITAR certification
best_when: You have large volumes of data to store and read frequently, especially when egress costs from AWS S3 are a pain point
avoid_when: You need deep AWS ecosystem integration, lifecycle policies as sophisticated as S3, or compliance certifications beyond SOC2/GDPR

auth.notes: AWS Signature V4 compatible (access key + secret key); uses same auth pattern as AWS S3 — boto3 and other S3 SDKs work by changing the endpoint URL

pricing.paid_tiers: $6.99/TB/month; no egress fees; no API request fees; minimum 1TB billed
pricing.notes: Minimum billing of 1TB even if you store less; strong value for >1TB use cases

performance.rate_limits: No published hard rate limits; consistent with S3-compatible services

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Inherits S3-compatible XML error responses; same patterns as AWS S3 so existing error handling code works
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: S3 PUT operations are idempotent by design; safe to retry on transient failures
known_agent_gotchas: Must change endpoint URL in boto3/SDK to point at Wasabi (not AWS) — easy to misconfigure
Minimum 1TB billing means it's not cost-effective for small storage needs
No native event notifications (no S3-equivalent Lambda triggers); must poll for changes
Region-specific endpoints required (e.g., s3.wasabisys.com vs s3.eu-central-1.wasabisys.com)

security_score_components.security_notes: AWS Sig V4 auth is industry standard; HTTPS enforced; bucket policies supported; no MFA delete equivalent
